title: crepis runcinata subsp. typica
intro: Crepis runcinata subsp. typica, also known as the sharp-leaved hawk's beard, is a species of plant in the dandelion family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and liver problems, due to its bitter compounds and flavonoids. The sharp-leaved hawk's beard is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential uses in treating gastrointestinal disorders and improving liver function. Herbal preparations of Crepis runcinata subsp. typica can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and overall well-being.
